title: Mechanical verification of Mondino-Sämann Def 3.6 LGH-convergence axioms on the Berezin / projection correspondence: closure of named gap G-B4
predecessor_sprints: Phase A.3' bridge construction (Bridge Theorem 6.4 with G-B2 and G-B4 named gaps)
L3b-2 Sub-Sprint D (Latremoliere propinquity assembly on K+ restriction)
L3e-P3 Phase A.2 epsilon-net + R1/R2/R3 candidates
task: Close Phase A.3' named gap G-B4: mechanically verify MS Def 3.6 axioms (extension property + forward density) on the Berezin/projection correspondence
summary: All four MS Def 3.6 axioms (i)-(iv) verify mechanically on the Berezin / projection correspondence built in Paper 45 Sub-Sprint D, including both weak and strong forms of (iv) timelike forward density. The convergence transport theorem (B4) closes at theorem-grade rigor, completing the named gap G-B4 of Phase A.3' Bridge Theorem 6.4.

axiom_i_cardinality_matching.ms_statement: |S_n| = |S| for all n >= n_0, where S and S_n are finite epsilon-nets for A and A_n
axiom_i_cardinality_matching.krein_side_analog: epsilon-net cardinality at fixed admissible cutoff (n_max(k), N_t(k), T(k)) is independent of n, so |S_n| = |S| trivially for all n
axiom_i_cardinality_matching.stronger_than_ms_note: Krein-side verification holds for all n, not just large n
axiom_i_cardinality_matching.load_bearing_dependency: Phase A.2' substrate (fixed admissible cutoff structure) + Sub-Sprint D §3 (propinquity constituents bounded uniformly)

axiom_ii_correspondence_distortion.ms_statement: For all n >= n_0, exists correspondence R_n of V(S_n) and V(S) with dis(R_n) -> 0 as n -> infinity
axiom_ii_correspondence_distortion.krein_side_analog: Berezin / projection correspondence R_{B,P}^n at scale gamma^joint (Definition 3.2) realizes the correspondence
axiom_ii_correspondence_distortion.verification_lemma: Lemma 4.2 (Vanishing distortion of Berezin / projection correspondence)
axiom_ii_correspondence_distortion.rate: O(log n_max / n_max + T/N_t) along admissible-scaling sequence
axiom_ii_correspondence_distortion.load_bearing_dependency: Sub-Sprint D §3.1 (reach_B bound) + §3.3 (height_B bound) at rate gamma^joint
axiom_ii_correspondence_distortion.substantive_new_content: Constructive realization (Berezin/projection pair) of MS abstract correspondence; rate identified as master Mellin engine M1 signature 4/pi at leading SU(2) order

axiom_iii_extension_property.ms_statement: For all l in N, each 1/l-net in the limit can be enlarged to include a 1/(l+1)-net while preserving convergence of the vertices, with extension f_{n'}^{l+1} satisfying dis(f_{n'}^{l+1}) <= dis(f_{n'}^l) for some n' >= n
axiom_iii_extension_property.krein_side_analog: Joint Berezin map at level l+1 (Plancherel cutoff n_max(l+1), N_t(l+1)) extends joint Berezin map at level l (cutoff n_max(l), N_t(l)), with bit-identical Plancherel weights on common support
axiom_iii_extension_property.load_bearing_dependency: Sub-Sprint C §1.2 joint Plancherel symbol factorization (tensor-product structure with bit-identical intrinsic weights)
axiom_iii_extension_property.substantive_new_content: Identification of Plancherel-nested Berezin structure as the structural realization of MS extension property; verification is mechanical because the structures align by construction
verification_steps: Step 1: Plancherel nesting (joint symbol factorizes; supports nested; weights bit-identical on common support)
Step 2: Berezin nesting (level-(l+1) Berezin map is level-l Berezin map plus additional Plancherel terms)
Step 3: Vertex-set extension (V(S^{l+1}) contains V(S^l) via Plancherel cutoff refinement)
Step 4: Distortion non-increase (joint rate monotone-decreasing along admissible-scaling sequence)

axiom_iv_weak_forward_density.ms_statement: For each x in A \ V (V = union of vertex sets), exists sequence (x_k) in V with x_k <= x_{k+1} <= x and x_k -> x
axiom_iv_weak_forward_density.krein_side_analog: For each character omega-hat on bridge image not in vertex set V = union_l V(S^l), exists modular-flow orbit discretization (omega-hat_j) with omega-hat_j <= omega-hat_{j+1} <= omega-hat and omega-hat_j -> omega-hat in chronological topology
axiom_iv_weak_forward_density.verification_lemma: Lemma 4.4 (Forward density via modular flow orbit)
axiom_iv_weak_forward_density.verification_status: VERIFIED at theorem-grade rigor with structural-care note
axiom_iv_weak_forward_density.load_bearing_dependency: Phase A.3' R3 modular-flow orbit structure + Phase A.2' §2.3 weak-* metrization (chronological topology finer than weak-*)
axiom_iv_weak_forward_density.structural_care_note: Forward density holds in the chronological topology of the bridge image (modular-precedence topology), not just in the weak-* Gelfand topology. By Phase A.2' weak-* metrization, weak-* is finer than chronological so chronological-density implies weak-* density; the reverse is not automatic but the chronological-density side is what MS requires.
axiom_iv_weak_forward_density.substantive_new_content: Modular-flow orbit discretization as the natural Krein-side analog of MS Cauchy-grid forward density (cf. MS Example 3.13)

axiom_iv_strong_timelike_forward_density.ms_statement: For each x in A \ V, exists sequence (x_k) in V with x_k << x_{k+1} << x (strict timelike precedence) and x_k -> x
axiom_iv_strong_timelike_forward_density.krein_side_analog: Strict modular precedence omega-hat_j << omega-hat_{j+1} corresponds to strictly positive modular-flow increment t_{j+1} - t_j > 0, which transports to strict ell^L-positivity via Connes-Rovelli identification ell^L = kappa_g * t > 0
axiom_iv_strong_timelike_forward_density.verification_status: VERIFIED automatically on the bridge image
axiom_iv_strong_timelike_forward_density.load_bearing_dependency: Phase A.3' Def 6.3(4) ell^L(omega-hat, omega-hat') = kappa_g * tau_mod(omega-hat, omega-hat') with strict positivity on modular orbits
axiom_iv_strong_timelike_forward_density.note: The 'strong' qualifier in MS Def 3.6 is automatic on the bridge image because strict modular precedence is the natural structure of modular flow

convergence_transport_theorem.theorem_label: Theorem 5.1 (B4 convergence transport — closure of Phase A.3' G-B4)
convergence_transport_theorem.statement: If a sequence of Krein pointed proper QMS converges in the Krein-side metametric topology of Phase A.2' Theorem 4.2-K (Đ^K(X^K_n, X^K_infinity) -> 0), then the bridge images under the Wick-rotation functor W converge in the Mondino-Sämann pointed LorGH topology of MS Def 3.12 (strong sense, with timelike forward density)
proof_structure: Step 1: Per-cover LGH convergence at each k (via four-axiom verification + admissible-scaling)
Step 2: pLGH convergence via MS Def 3.12 (per-cover LGH convergence at every k)
Step 3: Strong sense via Lemma 4.4-strong (timelike forward density automatic)

riemannian_limit_corollary.label: Corollary 5.2 (Riemannian-limit consistency)
riemannian_limit_corollary.statement: At N_t = 1, the convergence transport reduces bit-exactly to Paper 38's SU(2) propinquity convergence with rate 4/pi (Paper 38 §L2 quantitative rate)
riemannian_limit_corollary.load_bearing_falsifier: Riemannian-limit recovery is the load-bearing falsifier; the bit-exact reduction confirms the Lorentzian convergence reduces to proven Paper 38 convergence at N_t = 1

bridge_theorem_status_post_g_b4.label: Phase A.3' Bridge Theorem 6.4 (Krein–Mondino–Sämann Bridge), post-G-B4 closure

properties_status.B2_reverse_triangle_inequality: on-orbit case theorem-grade; off-orbit case proof-sketch with named gap G-B2 remaining
properties_status.B3_pre_compactness_inheritance: theorem-grade (Phase A.3' §6 via Paper 44 prop=2 + Sub-Sprint D cardinality bound)
properties_status.B4_convergence_transport: theorem-grade via Theorem 5.1 of this memo (G-B4 CLOSED)

bridge_theorem_status_post_g_b4.remaining_named_gap: G-B2 only (super-additivity of off-orbit Wick-rotated modular flow)

aggregate_g_b4_verdict.rationale: All four MS Def 3.6 axioms verified mechanically at theorem-grade rigor; both weak and strong forms of (iv) hold; no structural obstruction; mechanical-verification character of G-B4 confirmed
aggregate_g_b4_verdict.bridge_theorem_implication: B4 closes cleanly; bridge theorem now has only one remaining named gap (G-B2)

substantive_new_content_summary.headline: The MS Def 3.6 axioms (i)-(iv) match the structural ingredients of Paper 45 Sub-Sprint C/D one-to-one

structural_alignments.i_cardinality: ε-net cardinality is cutoff-dependent not sequence-dependent ← Sub-Sprint D fixed-cutoff structure
structural_alignments.ii_distortion: propinquity constituents reach_B + height_B at rate gamma^joint ← Sub-Sprint D §3
structural_alignments.iii_extension: Plancherel-nested Berezin structure with bit-identical weights on common support ← Sub-Sprint C §1.2
structural_alignments.iv_forward_density: modular-flow orbit discretization in chronological topology ← Phase A.3' R3 modular-flow framework

substantive_new_content_summary.durable_insight: When the bridge functor is constructed correctly, the MS axioms verify mechanically because the Krein-side structure is exactly the right shape for the MS structure to apply. The verification is mechanical because the structures align by construction, not by happenstance.
substantive_new_content_summary.most_surprising_finding: MS Def 3.6(iii) extension property is structurally Plancherel nesting in disguise — the Berezin map at finer cutoff is the Berezin map at coarser cutoff PLUS additional Plancherel terms with bit-identical weights on the common support. This is the substantive structural identification.

primary_next_sprint.label: Phase A.4'-C (GeoVac wedge instantiation of the bridge)
primary_next_sprint.target: Apply W to specific GeoVac wedge PPQMS at panel cells (n_max, N_t) in {(2,3), (3,5), (4,7)} and verify structural correspondence row-by-row
primary_next_sprint.deliverable: GeoVac wedge synthetic compactness corollary at theorem-grade rigor

alternative_or_parallel.label: Phase A.4'-D (G-B2 super-additivity proof)
alternative_or_parallel.target: Close the remaining named gap (G-B2) via Paper 42 four-witness theorem transport
alternative_or_parallel.deliverable: Theorem-grade super-additivity of off-orbit Wick-rotated modular flow
alternative_or_parallel.scheduling_note: Parallel to A.4'-C is reasonable; G-B2 is structurally deeper than G-B4

do_not_pursue_now.label: Q1' strong-form bridge (Phase A.5'+ or Paper 48 §8)
do_not_pursue_now.rationale: K+-weak-form bridge is the right scope; strong-form requires either K+-cone-of-enlarged-substrate (where K+ preservation may fail for chirality-flipping generators) or non-commutative MS extension (substantial NCG-research target)

total_estimated_end_to_end_post_a4prime_: ~5-6.5 months (slightly tighter than Phase A.3' estimate of 6.5-8.5 months due to G-B4 closing faster than upper bound)
